label: Sentiment rebounded in early June as gasoline-price pressure eased.
interpretation: The preliminary June survey showed a rebound from May while inflation expectations remained elevated.

label: Year-ahead inflation expectations edged down but stayed high.
interpretation: The one-year inflation outlook slipped from May, which supports a modest sentiment rebound but keeps cost-of-living risk in the forecast.

label: Current conditions and expectations both improved from May.
interpretation: June gains were broad enough to lift both current conditions and expectations, matching the weekly path's gradual improvement.

label: June sentiment improved from May but remained historically weak.
interpretation: The June monthly forecast is shaped by a rebound from a depressed May base, not by a full normalization of household sentiment.

label: Inflation expectations eased only partially in June.
interpretation: Lower inflation expectations help the June forecast, while still-elevated readings limit the upside.

label: Fuel-cost relief was a visible support for June sentiment.
interpretation: News coverage of easing gas prices aligns with the model's small positive June revision.

label: EU and euro-area consumer confidence continued recovering in June.
interpretation: The flash indicator rose by 1.2 points in the EU and 1.3 points in the euro area versus May, but stayed below its long-run average.

label: Euro-area confidence improved to -17.7 from -19.0.
interpretation: The improvement supports a less negative weekly path, while the level still points to weak household confidence.

label: The June reading remained well below its long-term average.
interpretation: The negative level keeps the EU27 nowcast cautious despite the month-on-month improvement.

label: June EU27 confidence improved from May but remained negative.
interpretation: The monthly path reflects a less negative June reading after May weakness, with confidence still below normal.

label: Euro-area sentiment stayed below its long-run average.
interpretation: The forecast improves modestly, but the level remains cautious because households still report weak confidence.

label: Inflation and growth uncertainty continue to weigh on households.
interpretation: The June forecast keeps the rebound small because household sentiment is still sensitive to prices and growth risks.

label: Japan's May consumer confidence index rose to 33.6.
interpretation: The latest official release showed a 1.4 point improvement from April, setting a firmer base for the June weekly path.

label: Employment and income-growth perceptions improved in May.
interpretation: Improvement in employment and income-growth subindices supports the mild upward weekly path.

label: Sentiment remained below the neutral 50 threshold.
interpretation: The latest reading improved but remained pessimistic, so the Japan forecast is shown as a cautious firming rather than a breakout.

label: Japan confidence strengthened from April into May.
interpretation: The June monthly forecast carries forward the improvement seen in the latest official confidence release.

label: Income-growth and employment perceptions supported the rebound.
interpretation: Better income-growth and employment readings point to firmer household sentiment entering June.

label: Confidence improved, but the level remained cautious.
interpretation: The Japan monthly path shows a mild strengthening rather than a sharp recovery because sentiment remains below neutral.
